TITLE: spike: SAM 3 availability + API verification
LABELS: roadmap, type:spike, size:S, phase:D-sam3
---
## Context

Milestone D plans two SAM 3 features: native text-prompt segmentation reusing the DINO review workflow (#50) and video object tracking (#51). Both designs hinge on facts we have not verified: whether SAM 3 is consumable through Ultralytics (the project's chosen integration layer — ADR-002 "Use Ultralytics for SAM Integration", `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md:13`) or only through `facebookresearch/sam3`; what the exact text-prompt and video-tracking APIs look like; model sizes; licensing; and minimum dependency versions against our current stack.

This is a **time-boxed, research-only** spike. **NO product code.** The deliverable is a report committed as a **draft ADR** so the findings are versioned where D2/D3 implementers will look for them.

## Current state

- SAM 2 runs in-process via Ultralytics: `src/digitalsreeni_image_annotator/inference/sam_utils.py` — model registry `MODEL_NAMES`/`MODEL_FILES` (lines 43-63), lazy `from ultralytics import SAM` load in `_load_model_blocking` (346-355), QThread wrapping in `_run_sync` (244-287, ADR-013), device resolution via `core/torch_utils.py::resolve_torch_device`.
- Text-prompted segmentation today is a two-stage pipeline: Grounding-DINO boxes (`inference/dino_utils.py`, HF Transformers, `detect` at lines 141-147) → SAM mask refinement (`controllers/dino_controller.py::run_dino_detection_single`, lines 192-342). SAM 3's native text prompting would collapse this to one stage.
- Ultralytics version is stated inconsistently in the repo (CLAUDE.md "8.3.27", `setup.py` `>=8.0.0`) — reconciled by #38; this spike must state the minimum version actually required for SAM 3, against that baseline.
- ADR numbering: `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` currently tops out at ADR-029. Next free number: **ADR-030** at the time of writing — verify the file's max (C1 may have taken 030 by then) and take the next free one.
- Relevant constraints to test findings against: Python 3.10+, PyQt6, torch via Ultralytics, CPU-only users exist (`core/torch_utils.py` has explicit CPU-fallback handling; CLAUDE.md warns "SAM 2 large crashes on limited RAM").

## Proposed design

Produce `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` → new draft ADR titled "SAM 3 integration path" with **Status: Proposed**, answering every question below with sources (links + version numbers + date checked). Where a question cannot be answered definitively, say so explicitly and state what would resolve it — do not guess.

### Questions the ADR must answer

1. **Distribution**: Is SAM 3 supported in Ultralytics (which release? which model names/weight files, e.g. an equivalent of `SAM("sam3_…")`)? Or is `facebookresearch/sam3` (or an HF Transformers port) the only route? If both exist, compare maturity. State the recommendation and whether it upholds or supersedes ADR-002.
2. **Text-prompt image API**: exact Python call for open-vocabulary/"concept" text prompting on a single image — entry-point class, method signature, prompt format (free text vs. phrase list), returned structure (masks/boxes/scores and their exact types), and whether per-class confidence thresholds are supported natively (our DINO UI exposes box/text/NMS thresholds per class — map which of these have SAM 3 equivalents).
3. **Video tracking API**: exact API for initialising a video session, seeding with a mask/box/point/text on an arbitrary frame, and propagating forward AND backward. Is it streaming (frame-by-frame generator) or whole-video? What per-frame state does the predictor keep, and what is the documented/observed memory behaviour on long videos (this feeds D3's verify-first item)? Can it consume frames supplied incrementally as numpy arrays (our C1 `VideoHandler` decodes on demand), or does it demand a video file path?
4. **Models & download**: available checkpoint sizes (MB) and variants; download mechanics (auto-download like Ultralytics SAM 2 into a working-dir cache, HF hub snapshot like our DINO models at `inference/dino_utils.py:335-360`, or manual gated download); any auth/license-acceptance gate on the weights.
5. **License**: exact license of code AND weights (SAM-model licenses have differed from repo licenses before); is it compatible with this project's usage and redistribution-of-outputs expectations? Quote the license name and the restrictive clauses, if any.
6. **Minimum versions**: minimum ultralytics / torch / torchvision / transformers / Python versions required, checked against our floor (Python 3.10, and the #38-reconciled ultralytics bound). Flag any conflict (e.g. "needs torch ≥ X which drops CUDA capability Y" — cf. the Pascal-GPU fallback handling in `core/torch_utils.py`).
7. **CPU fallback**: does SAM 3 run on CPU at all; rough expected latency class for a single 1080p text-prompt inference (from docs/issues/benchmarks — cite, don't invent); is there a smaller variant recommended for CPU users?
8. **Decision for D2**: one paragraph — does D2 mirror `SAMUtils` via Ultralytics (preferred), or does it need a new backend (HF Transformers like `DINOUtils`, or a vendored `sam3` dependency)? List the resulting new entries for `pyproject.toml`/`requirements.txt`.

## Implementation steps

1. Branch `spike/sam3-availability`.
2. Time-box: ~1 day of research. Sources to check, in order: Ultralytics docs + GitHub release notes/changelog (search "SAM3"/"SAM 3"); `github.com/facebookresearch/sam3` (README, model card, license file, issues on memory/CPU); Hugging Face model hub entries; the SAM 3 paper/model card for variant sizes.
3. Optional and sandboxed: a throwaway script OUTSIDE `src/` (scratch dir, not committed) may be used to confirm import paths/signatures against a pip-installed candidate. Nothing under `src/` changes; no new dependencies land in packaging files.
4. Write the draft ADR (next free number, Status: Proposed) with the Context/Decision/Consequences structure used by ADR-021+ in `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md`; put the eight answers under "Decision drivers" / findings subsections; every claim carries a source link and the date checked.
5. Cross-link: add a one-line pointer from `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` if a material risk is found (e.g. restrictive weight license, no CPU path).
6. Run the smoke tests (docs-only change, cheap insurance), run the senior-reviewer agent, open PR.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A new draft ADR exists in `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` with Status **Proposed**, using the next free ADR number (verified against the file at PR time).
- [ ] Each of questions 1-8 is answered in its own subsection, with at least one dated source link per answer; unanswerable points are explicitly marked "unresolved" with what would resolve them.
- [ ] The ADR ends with a concrete recommendation for D2 (integration route + dependency list + minimum versions) and a go/no-go note for D3's tracking API, including the memory-behaviour finding.
- [ ] ADR-002's status line is annotated if the recommendation deviates from Ultralytics (superseded-by pointer), left untouched otherwise.
- [ ] **Zero changes under `src/`, `tests/`, or packaging files** — `git diff --stat` shows docs only.
- [ ] Smoke tests green.

## Test plan

No product code, so no new tests. Verification is:

- `pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v` (guards against accidental source edits).
- `git diff --stat master` reviewed in the PR: only `docs/` (and optionally CLAUDE.md backlog-row deletion) touched.
- Senior-reviewer agent run on the ADR text itself (it reads diffs, not just code).

## Docs to update

- `docs/09_architecture_decisions.md` — the draft ADR IS the deliverable (next free number; **ADR-030** at time of writing, re-verify).
- `docs/11_risks_and_technical_debt.md` — only if a material licensing/memory/CPU risk is found.
- CLAUDE.md — delete this issue's backlog row in the same PR if present.

## Pitfalls & guardrails

- **NO product code** — the temptation to "just wire it up while I'm here" defeats the point: D2 is sized and sequenced assuming these facts arrive first. Any prototype code stays in scratch space and out of the commit.
- **Don't trust memory/training-data knowledge about SAM 3** — the model landscape moves faster than any knowledge cutoff. Every claim in the ADR must come from a fetched, dated source; if web access is unavailable in the implementation session, stop and say so rather than writing an ADR from recall.
- **Weights license ≠ code license** — check both, explicitly (question 5). Meta's SAM releases have used bespoke licenses for weights.
- **Version claims must name exact versions** ("supported since ultralytics X.Y.Z"), because #38 pins bounds from them.
- **Keep ADR status Proposed** — it is accepted/amended by the D2 PR, not by this spike.
- The repo's own inconsistency (CLAUDE.md 8.3.27 vs setup.py `>=8.0.0` vs `training/sam_trainer.py:7` mentioning 8.4.51) means "our current ultralytics version" must be read from the environment (`pip show ultralytics`), not from docs.

## Dependencies

- No blockers.
- Feeds (blocks): #50, #51 — both cite this ADR's answers.
- Related, not blocking: #38 (dependency reconciliation) — coordinate the minimum-version findings with its chosen bounds.

_Workflow: follow CLAUDE.md — feature branch, run smoke tests (`pytest tests/integration/test_smoke.py -v`), update arc42 docs, run the senior-reviewer agent before the PR._
